shell.brandDescription: Operational control for documents, risks, audits, CAPA, reviews, KPIs, and training.

page.label: Start Here
page.title: Implementation and readiness path
page.description: Use this page as the first stop for tenant setup: review the core readiness checklist, then use the optional implementation workspace when guided rollout is needed.

feedback.loadingCopy: Bringing in tenant rollout settings and objective guidance.
feedback.noWriteWorkspace: You do not have permission to update the implementation workspace.
feedback.noWriteChecklist: You do not have permission to update the rollout checklist.
feedback.loadError: Implementation workspace could not be loaded.
feedback.saveError: Implementation workspace could not be saved.

intro.title: Start here
intro.copy: Use this page to orient a new tenant quickly. The readiness checklist stays available for everyone with workspace access, and the implementation workspace can remain optional for mature companies.

diagram.title: How the modules interact across the IMS
diagram.copy: Use this diagram as the quickest orientation view. The process register stays at the centre, planning modules shape the system, operational modules run it, assurance modules test it, and improvement modules close the loop.
diagram.improvementBar: Continual improvement across the integrated management system
diagram.coreCopy: Use the process register as the shared reference point for ownership, interfaces, linked evidence, and cross-module follow-up.

plan.title: Plan the system
plan.copy: Set context, obligations, risks, and measures before daily operation starts.

do.title: Run the process
do.copy: Use documents, process controls, training, and supplier inputs to operate consistently.

check.title: Check performance
check.copy: Test the system through audits, incidents, hazards, and environmental assurance records.

act.title: Improve and decide
act.copy: Turn findings into NCR, CAPA, actions, and management review decisions.

readiness.title: Core setup checklist
readiness.copy: Walk through these essentials in order so the tenant has a clear first-use path before deeper rollout work begins.

companyProfile.label: Company profile
companyProfile.hint: Confirm tenant identity, numbering defaults, and system setup.

usersRoles.label: Users and roles
usersRoles.hint: Set access, ownership, and who will operate each module.

processMap.label: Process map
processMap.hint: Define the main processes and how they connect across the system.

controlledDocuments.label: Controlled documents
controlledDocuments.hint: Load the policies, procedures, forms, and work instructions needed for operation.

risks.label: Risks
risks.hint: Capture key business, compliance, QHSE, and operational risks early.

audits.label: Audits
audits.hint: Set the audit programme and prepare how effectiveness will be checked.

ncrCapaActions.label: NCR/CAPA/actions
ncrCapaActions.hint: Make sure nonconformities, corrective action, and follow-up ownership are ready.

kpis.label: KPIs
kpis.hint: Define the measures and thresholds that show whether the system is working.

managementReview.label: Management review
managementReview.hint: Prepare the leadership review path for decisions, resources, and improvement.

guidance.flowCopy: Company profile -> Users -> Processes -> Documents -> Risks -> Audits -> NCR/CAPA -> KPIs -> Management Review.
guidance.hiddenTitle: The implementation workspace is currently hidden for this tenant.
guidance.hiddenCopy: That is useful for companies that already have ISO implemented and only need the operational modules. This Start Here page still stays available for orientation, and you can turn the deeper rollout workspace back on later in

profile.title: Rollout profile
profile.copy: Keep the setup context short and visible so the tenant knows how this workspace is being used.

checklist.title: Rollout checkpoints
checklist.copy: High-level setup only. This should stay lighter than a project plan and simply show whether the basics are in place.

items.scopeContext: Define scope, context, and interested parties
items.policyDocuments: Approve policies and controlled document structure
items.objectivesKpis: Set objectives, targets, and KPI review ownership
items.processRisk: Map processes and assess key risks, hazards, and aspects
items.operationsTraining: Deploy operational controls, training, and provider controls
items.auditReview: Run internal audit and hold management review

objective.title: Objective establishment starter
objective.copy: Keep one practical starter objective here, then move the live measure and readings into KPIs.

placeholders.statement: Improve on-time supplier approval and reduce traceability gaps by year end.

pdca.title: Recommended module sequence
pdca.copy: Use PDCA as a suggested rollout order, not as another register to keep updating.

Plan.label: Plan
Plan.summary: Define the management system direction before pushing records into daily use.

Do.label: Do
Do.summary: Deploy controls, competence, and supplier management into daily operations.

Check.label: Check
Check.summary: Use evidence, incidents, and audits to test whether the system is performing as intended.

Act.label: Act
Act.summary: Drive improvement from findings, failures, changes, and management decisions.

modules.Context: Scope, issues, interested parties, and needs

page.label: Dashboard
page.title: Integrated Management System
page.description: A structured operational workspace for quality, environmental, and health and safety management.

watchlist.title: Areas needing attention
watchlist.copy: A quick view of the modules that currently need review.

breakdown.title: {{module}} breakdown
breakdown.copy: A compact view of the {{metric}} behind the main {{chart}}.

page.label: Clause 4
page.title: Context of Organization
page.description: Manage the issues, parties, and expectations that shape the management system.

empty.noAccessCopy: Your current role does not include context.read.
empty.noIssuesCopy: Start by adding internal or external issues.

headlines.open: Open context issues still need active review

narratives.open: Use the open issues as management inputs. Where needed, continue into risk assessment or process review so the issue has visible follow-up.
narratives.monitoring: Monitoring issues remain visible for review and can still be escalated into risks or process changes if the situation worsens.
narratives.stable: Recent context records are either resolved or not currently driving immediate management attention.

areas.title: Open one working area at a time
areas.copy: Choose the register you want to review, then work inside that area without losing the Clause 4 view.

internalIssues.label: Internal issues
internalIssues.copy: Operational, organizational, and culture factors inside the business.

externalIssues.label: External issues
externalIssues.copy: Regulatory, supplier, market, and stakeholder influences on the IMS.

interestedParties.label: Interested parties
interestedParties.copy: Customers, regulators, employees, suppliers, and other key stakeholders.

needs.label: Needs & expectations
needs.copy: The requirements and expectations that should stay visible in planning and review.

recent.title: Latest context records
recent.copy: Review the latest internal and external issues, then continue into risk or process follow-up where needed.

errors.load: Context dashboard could not be loaded.

descriptions.list: Review controlled documents and move them through a simple lifecycle: Draft, Review, Approved, and Obsolete.
descriptions.create: Create a controlled document and prepare it for review, approval, and future revision control.
descriptions.detail: Review document details, confirm lifecycle status, and keep evidence and actions together with the record.
descriptions.edit: Update document details without losing the controlled lifecycle and revision history.

list.title: Controlled document register
list.copy: Controlled documents are the live policies, procedures, forms, and instructions people use to run the system every day.
list.filtersCopy: Search by code, title, or type, then open a record for review or revision control.
list.emptyTitle: No documents match the current filter
list.emptyCopy: Adjust the search or create the first controlled document for this tenant.

form.copy: Set the document identity, owner, and lifecycle here. Evidence and actions stay in dedicated panels once the record exists.
form.title: Title

detail.lifecycleCopy: Move the record through review, approval, and obsolescence without opening the editor.
detail.evidenceCopy: Keep supporting files and related actions with the controlled record so lifecycle review and revision history stay easy to follow.

detailLifecycle.draft: Next control point: complete the revision note, assign ownership, and move the document into review when the draft is ready.
detailLifecycle.reviewWithApprove: Next control point: review the evidence trail and approve the document when the content and metadata are complete.
detailLifecycle.reviewWithoutApprove: Next control point: confirm evidence and revision details, then hand the document to an approver with documents.approve permission.
detailLifecycle.approved: Next control point: maintain review due dates and linked actions, and mark the record obsolete rather than deleting it when superseded.
detailLifecycle.obsolete: Next control point: keep the obsolete record for traceability and make sure the replacement live document is available in the register.

nextSteps.approved: Next: review evidence, confirm linked actions, and keep the review due date current.
nextSteps.review: Next: review the lifecycle step, confirm evidence is complete, and move the document toward approval.
nextSteps.default: Next: confirm lifecycle details, review evidence, and keep linked actions up to date.

reviewDue.overdueCopy: This controlled document passed its review due date on {{date}}. Review the content, confirm relevance, and either revise or reapprove it.
reviewDue.soonCopy: This document is due for review on {{date}}. Confirm the owner, evidence, and revision note before the review date passes.

messages.noPermission: You do not have permission to update documents.
messages.confirmDeleteDraft: Delete this draft document? Approved and obsolete documents cannot be deleted.
messages.confirmObsolete: Mark this approved document as obsolete? This preserves traceability instead of deleting the record.

hints.draft: Use Draft while the content is being prepared. This is the working version before review and approval.
hints.review: Use Review when the content is ready to be checked and approved. Keep the revision note and supporting evidence current before approval.
hints.approved: Approved is the controlled live version in use. Keep the effective date and review due date current so periodic review stays visible.
hints.obsolete: Obsolete keeps the old version available for traceability after it has been replaced by a new live document.

descriptions.list: A calmer register for assessed risks and opportunities, ownership, target follow-up, and linked actions.
descriptions.create: Capture a new risk or opportunity in a dedicated page without mixing the register and editor.
descriptions.detail: Review the assessment, current actions, and evidence in one focused detail view.
descriptions.edit: Update the assessment and action plan in a dedicated edit workflow.

list.title: Risk register
list.copy: A clear enterprise register for risks, opportunities, treatment ownership, linked actions, and live scoring.
list.filtersCopy: Search by title or category, then open the record for assessment, actions, and evidence.
list.loadingCopy: Refreshing current assessment and treatment data.
list.emptyTitle: No records match the current filter
list.emptyCopy: Adjust the search or create the first risk or opportunity entry for this tenant.

form.stillLinkedTitle: Still linked to the source issue
form.title: Title
form.starterCategoryHint: Starter categories switch with {{entity}} type. You can still enter a custom category.

intro.risk: Separate the initial risk, mitigation planning, and residual risk review so the assessment reads like a real IMS risk register.
intro.opportunity: Separate the initial opportunity, realization actions, and residual opportunity review so the record is easy to follow.

likelihoodHint.opportunity: Scale 1-5: 1 unlikely to realize, 5 very likely to realize.

detail.evidenceCopy: Keep the source issue, process context, mitigation actions, and supporting evidence aligned to this record.

page.descriptionList: Manage internal and supplier audits with clear planning, checklist execution, findings, and follow-up.
page.descriptionCreate: Set up the audit plan, agenda, and meeting notes first, then move into the checklist.
page.descriptionDetail: Review the audit plan, complete the checklist, decide the next step for each finding, and finish the close-out.
page.descriptionEdit: Update audit metadata without mixing it with execution details.

actions.saveAndPrepareChecklist: Save audit and prepare AI checklist

list.title: Internal and supplier audits
list.copy: Plan audits, work through the checklist, record findings, and decide the right follow-up in one place.
list.loadingCopy: Refreshing current audit plans, checklist progress, and findings.

form.copy: Set the audit plan first. Internal audits can preload ISO-based questions, while supplier audits can stay simple and custom.

placeholders.title: Purchasing process audit
placeholders.scope: Process, site, supplier, or function under audit
placeholders.objectives: What should this audit confirm, test, or improve?
placeholders.criteria: List the requirements, procedures, clauses, or standards being audited.
placeholders.auditeeArea: Process owner, department, supplier, or site being audited
placeholders.agenda: Opening meeting, checklist walk-through, evidence review, close-out discussion
placeholders.openingMeetingNotes: Capture opening meeting notes, attendees, and scope confirmation.
placeholders.closingMeetingNotes: Capture closing meeting notes, conclusions, and commitments.
placeholders.summary: Summarize the planned audit focus and expected outcome.

procedureGuidance.title: Procedure-based audit setup
procedureGuidance.copy: When the audit focuses on a controlled procedure, select the process and linked document first so the checklist is built from the right source.
procedureGuidance.noProcedures: No linked controlled procedures are available for the selected process yet.
procedureGuidance.selectOne: Select one linked procedure to build a targeted checklist.
procedureGuidance.ready: The linked procedure is ready to drive the audit checklist.

model.title: How this audit will run
model.copy: Use the plan first, then move through the checklist, findings, and close-out in one guided flow.

programmePlanning.title: Programme planning
programmePlanning.copy: Keep the audit tied to the right annual programme or supplier review cycle.

scopePlanning.title: Scope planning
scopePlanning.copy: Define the process, department, site, or supplier before fieldwork starts.

aiChecklist.title: AI checklist support
aiChecklist.copy: Internal audits can preload ISO-aligned questions based on the selected standard.

meetingTemplates.title: Meeting templates
meetingTemplates.copy: Prepare opening and closing meeting notes before execution begins.

errors.selectStandard: Select an ISO standard for internal audits.

nextSteps.default: Next: continue the audit from the current workflow step.
nextSteps.completed: Next: review the completed audit record, its findings, and the formal report output.
nextSteps.review: Next: review findings, complete the close-out, and then finish the audit from the review step.
nextSteps.conduct: Next: continue the checklist, record findings where needed, and return to the review step when the checklist is complete.

workflow.step1: Step 1: confirm scope and audit setup
workflow.step3Findings: Step 3: decide the next step for each finding

workflowCopy.openRecord: Select an audit to continue the workflow.
workflowCopy.completed: The audit record is already complete. You can still review findings, linked CAPA, lighter audit actions, and the final PDF report.
workflowCopy.plan: Check the programme, scope, objectives, criteria, and meeting notes first. Once the setup reads correctly, start the audit and move into the checklist.
workflowCopy.conduct: Work through the checklist question by question. Record findings only where the requirement is not met, then keep moving through the audit.
workflowCopy.review: Stay in the findings trail until every gap has a clear next step. Minor and major findings move into CAPA; observations and opportunities can use a lighter audit action or close directly.
workflowCopy.closeout: All findings have a clear next step. Record the conclusion, recommendations, completion date, and auditor to finish the audit without reopening the checklist.

plan.title: Programme, scope, and readiness
plan.copy: Confirm the audit programme, scope type, criteria, agenda, and meeting structure before starting fieldwork.

empty.openingMeeting: No opening meeting notes prepared yet.
empty.closingMeeting: No closing meeting notes prepared yet.

pack.internalCopy: Use the programme, agenda, and opening or closing notes to issue the audit properly before the checklist starts.
pack.supplierCopy: Supplier audits should still start with a simple agenda, opening discussion, and planned close-out summary before questions are assessed.

annualProgramme.title: Annual audit programme
annualProgramme.copy: Use the programme field to show which annual plan or audit cycle this audit belongs to before it moves into execution.

scopePlanning.title: Department, process, or whole-company planning
scopePlanning.copy: The scope type makes it explicit whether the audit is company-wide, department-based, process-based, site-based, or supplier-focused.

meetingStructure.title: Meeting structure
meetingStructure.copy: Opening and closing meeting notes let the audit record show how the agenda was issued, how the audit was opened, and how the results were closed out.

conduct.copy: Answer each question in order. If a requirement is not met, choose No, record the finding, then continue the audit from the same question.
conduct.readOnlyCopy: This audit has been completed. You can still review findings and evidence, but checklist responses can no longer be changed.

currentClauseHelper.supplier: Answer each supplier audit question, record evidence, and add a finding only when a requirement is not met.
currentClauseHelper.internal: Answer each question. If a requirement is not met, record a finding and continue to the next question.

conduct.emptyInternalCopy: Checklist questions will appear here once the audit template is available.
conduct.emptySupplierCopy: Add the first supplier audit question to begin the audit.

completion.title: Next step: review findings
completion.copy: All checklist questions are answered. Open Review findings now, decide the next step for each gap, then complete the audit close-out last.

findingNextStep.needed: Record the finding before moving on so the failed requirement has a complete audit trail.
findingNextStep.closed: This finding route is already closed for the question. Review it if needed, or continue with the next question.
findingNextStep.capaLinked: Finding and CAPA are already linked. Review the linked CAPA or continue with the next question.
findingNextStep.capaNeeded: Finding recorded. CAPA still needs to be raised for this nonconformity before the route is complete.
findingNextStep.lightRoute: Finding recorded. Review the lighter follow-up route or continue with the next question.

findingPrompt.titleClosed: Finding route is already closed for this question
findingPrompt.titleCapaLinked: Finding and CAPA already linked for this question
findingPrompt.titleCapaNeeded: Finding recorded: CAPA still needed for this question
findingPrompt.copyNeeded: The No answer has been recorded. Now capture the finding title, gap description, and severity so the review step has a complete audit trail.
findingPrompt.copyClosed: This failed answer already has a finding with a closed route. Use Review Finding if you need to reopen that trail before continuing.
findingPrompt.copyCapaLinked: This failed answer already has a linked finding and CAPA. Use Review Finding or Open CAPA if you need to return to that trail before continuing.
findingPrompt.copyCapaNeeded: This failed answer already has a finding, but the nonconformity still needs CAPA before the route is complete. Use Review Finding to continue that decision.
findingPrompt.copyLightRoute: This failed answer already has a lighter finding route. Use Review Finding if you need to review action or closure before continuing.

findingModal.title: Record audit finding
findingModal.descriptionHelper: Write the real situation first, then use AI only if you want help turning it into cleaner audit wording.
findingModal.checklistCopy: 1. Write a short note in your own words. 2. Use AI only if you want cleaner wording. 3. Choose severity. 4. Save the finding. 5. Decide the next step later in Review findings.

review.title: Finish finding routes, then close the audit
review.copy: Keep the chain simple: review each finding, decide the next step inside that finding, then record the audit close-out last.
review.incompleteCopy: Answer all required checklist questions before the audit can move into final completion.

focus.default: Current focus: review each finding one at a time
focus.copyDefault: Select a finding, decide whether it should close, move into an audit action, or move into CAPA, then continue to the next finding.

guidance.routeComplete: Route is complete in the audit record.
guidance.continueLinkedCapa: Continue the linked CAPA, then return here only when you need to review closure.
guidance.linkedAction: A lighter audit action is already linked to this finding.
guidance.actionPrepared: Lighter follow-up is already prepared for this finding below.
guidance.capaNeeded: This finding needs CAPA before the next step is complete.
guidance.lightRoute: Choose a lighter action or close the finding directly when satisfied.

review.traceabilityCopy: Follow the chain from checklist evidence to finding, then into {{capaText}} and {{actionText}}.

guidance.completeChecklist: Complete all checklist questions before the audit can be finished.
guidance.unresolvedFindings: Each finding still needs a clear next step before this audit should move into final close-out.
guidance.completeFields: Record the conclusion, recommendations, completion date, and auditor before finishing the audit.
guidance.ready: The audit record can be completed now. Findings, actions, and NCR follow-up can continue after audit completion without reopening the audit itself.

attention.needsAttention: This audit currently needs management attention.
attention.underControl: This audit is currently under control.
attention.loading: Attention guidance appears after the audit is loaded.
attention.controlledCopy: Audit timing, checklist execution, and findings follow-up are controlled enough for routine oversight.

page.label: Assurance
page.title: Actions
page.description: Track assigned follow-up from any module in one place.

focused.copy: Review the action details above, then update status or return to the source record.
focused.packageSummaryTitle: Source record kept in a higher package
focused.packageSummaryCopy: This action stays linked to {{module}}. The full source module is included from {{package}} upward, so this summary stays visible here without breaking the action workflow.

register.title: Assigned follow-up
register.copy: Track open work, ownership, and due dates from audits, CAPA, incidents, risks, and other modules.

empty.loadingCopy: Refreshing assigned follow-up, owners, and due dates.
empty.noneTitle: No actions match the current filters
empty.noneCopy: Adjust the filters or open a source record to create a new action.

headlines.ownerNeeded: Some actions still need an owner
headlines.active: Follow-up is active across the system
headlines.underControl: Assigned follow-up is currently under control

narratives.overdue: Start with overdue actions first. They represent assigned follow-up that has already missed the committed due date.
narratives.ownerNeeded: Some actions are still open or in progress without a named owner. Assign responsibility before the next review cycle so follow-up does not stall.
narratives.active: Use this page to track assigned follow-up from any module so ownership, due dates, and status stay visible in one place.
narratives.underControl: Current action records are either complete or not yet needing extra intervention.

headlines.needsAttention: This action currently needs management attention.
headlines.underControl: This action is currently under control.

narratives.underControl: The action has either been completed, cancelled, or still has enough control around due date and ownership.

messages.noPermissionUpdate: You do not have permission to update actions.
messages.deleteConfirm: Delete action "{{title}}"? Completed actions are protected.

descriptions.list: A focused training list for course setup, due dates, and completion progress.
descriptions.create: Create a course in a dedicated page instead of mixing course setup with assignments.
descriptions.detail: Review assignments, completion, and evidence in one clean course detail page.
descriptions.edit: Update the course definition without the noise of assignment management.

list.title: Training courses
list.copy: A focused list of courses with delivery method, due date, and overall completion.

form.copy: Keep the course definition separate from assignments and completion evidence.
form.guidanceCopy: Define the expected audience, delivery method, and due date so the course can be assigned and later evidenced as completed.
form.guidanceNote: Training becomes audit-ready when assignment records clearly show due date, completion status, and evidence summary by person.

assignments.title: Assignments
assignments.copy: Assign training, track completion, and keep evidence of competence by user.
assignments.formNote: When an assignment is marked complete, the evidence summary should explain what demonstrated competence: attendance, test result, observation, or signed record.

readiness.headlineGathering: Competence evidence is still being gathered
readiness.narrativeDefault: Assignments and evidence will appear once the course is saved.
readiness.narrativeComplete: All current assignments are complete. Keep the evidence summaries clear enough to demonstrate competence during review or audit.
readiness.narrativeOverdue: At least one learner is past due. Management follow-up should confirm whether the course is delayed, reassigned, or needs additional support.
readiness.narrativeGathering: Learners are progressing through the course, but the record still needs completion evidence before competence can be treated as verified.
readiness.narrativeAssigned: The course is defined, but ongoing evidence depends on assigning people and maintaining assignment updates.
readiness.hintDefault: Save the course first, then assign people and record their evidence of completion.
readiness.hintComplete: Next step: keep refresher timing and reassignment under control if this competence needs periodic renewal.
readiness.hintOverdue: Next step: review the overdue learners and update due dates or status before the record falls behind further.
readiness.hintAssigned: Next step: keep assignment notes and evidence summaries current so auditors can see how competence was confirmed.

assignmentGuidance.overdue: Assignment is overdue. Update the learner status or due date and record the current follow-up.
assignmentGuidance.completed: Completion should be supported by a short evidence summary.
assignmentGuidance.inProgress: Record how the learner is progressing and what evidence is still expected.
assignmentGuidance.assigned: Assignment created. Add evidence once the learner completes the course.

messages.loadDetailsFailed: Training details could not be loaded.
messages.loadListFailed: Training courses could not be loaded.

descriptions.list: Keep significant environmental aspects, impacts, and linked controls visible without building a duplicate risk workflow.
descriptions.create: Record the activity, aspect, impact, current controls, and review owner in one lightweight register.
descriptions.detail: Review the aspect, environmental impact, current controls, and linked IMS records.
descriptions.edit: Update the aspect record while keeping linked processes, risks, and actions in their original modules.

list.copy: Track environmental aspects, impacts, and review ownership in one visible register.
list.loadingCopy: Refreshing aspect records, impacts, and linked controls.
list.emptyCopy: Create the first aspect to start tracking environmental exposure visibly in the IMS.

placeholders.activity: Chemical transfer, waste handling, dispatch loading
placeholders.impact: Describe the resulting impact if the aspect is not controlled.
placeholders.controlSummary: Summarize the key operational controls currently applied.

guidance.structured: This aspect record already shows the activity, environmental exposure, resulting impact, and review ownership.
guidance.default: Record the activity, the environmental aspect it creates, the resulting impact, and who reviews the current controls so the exposure stays visible.

review.noRecord: Save the aspect first, then link the process, risk, or action records that show how it is controlled.
review.unlinked: This environmental aspect is recorded, but its control and follow-up are not yet visible through linked records.
review.partial: This aspect has some traceability, but it will be stronger once both the owning process and related risk are linked.
review.strong: This environmental aspect already shows where it is controlled and how follow-up is being managed.

headline.default: Next steps appear after the aspect is saved.
headline.risk: Link the related risk assessment next.
headline.action: Prepare an environmental follow-up action next.
headline.ready: This aspect is connected to the main control records.

copy.default: Save the aspect first, then decide whether the next step is process linkage, risk visibility, or follow-up action ownership.
copy.process: Link the process that owns the operational control first so this aspect is anchored in the live system flow.
copy.risk: This aspect is significant enough to justify visible risk linkage so environmental exposure and treatment stay reviewable.
copy.action: If control improvement, monitoring, or review follow-up is still needed, prepare an action so ownership and due dates stay visible in the Actions tracker.
copy.ready: The aspect already shows ownership, operational control, and follow-up visibility in one place.

links.title: Traceability hub
links.copy: Link the existing process, risk, and action records that already carry the live follow-up.

PROCESS.title: Linked processes
PROCESS.copy: Processes that own or apply the operational control for this aspect.
PROCESS.empty: Link the process that owns or applies the environmental control.

RISK.title: Linked risks
RISK.copy: Risks used to track the exposure or control weakness connected to this aspect.
RISK.empty: Link a risk if this aspect creates visible exposure or requires treatment.

ACTION.title: Linked actions
ACTION.copy: Follow-up actions already tracked in the global action register.
ACTION.empty: Link actions already being tracked for follow-up.

messages.noPermissionWrite: You do not have permission to edit environmental aspects.
messages.completeRequired: Complete the required environmental aspect fields.
messages.loadListFailed: Environmental aspects could not be loaded.
messages.loadDetailsFailed: Environmental aspect details could not be loaded.
messages.loadOwnersFailed: Environmental aspect owners could not be loaded.
messages.riskLinkNeeded: Related risk assessment still needs to be linked.

descriptions.list: Keep approved suppliers, contractors, and outsourced services visible without turning the app into a procurement system.
descriptions.create: Record the provider, annual evaluation, approval basis, and review ownership in one lightweight supplier-control register.
descriptions.detail: Review provider control position, annual evaluation, supplier-audit coverage, and linked IMS records.
descriptions.edit: Update the provider record while keeping audits, risks, obligations, and actions in their original modules.

list.copy: Track approved suppliers, contractors, and outsourced services in one controlled register.
list.loadingCopy: Refreshing approved providers, outsourced services, and review timing.
list.emptyCopy: Create the first provider to keep supplier and outsourced-service control visible in the IMS.

placeholders.suppliedScope: What product, service, or outsourced activity this provider supports.
placeholders.approvalBasis: Summarize why the provider is approved, conditionally accepted, or under review.

evaluation.title: Annual evaluation
evaluation.copy: Use one simple annual questionnaire so supplier review stays comparable across providers. Score each item from 1 to 5.

fields.evaluationSummaryPlaceholder: Summarize the main strengths, weaknesses, and any conditions from the annual provider review.

1.title: Weak
1.definition: Performance is not controlled. Repeated gaps or major concerns are visible.

2.title: Fragile
2.definition: Some control exists, but performance is inconsistent and needs prompt follow-up.

3.title: Acceptable
3.definition: The requirement is generally met, but follow-up or tighter consistency is still needed.

4.title: Controlled
4.definition: Performance is reliable and effective, with only minor improvement needed.

5.title: Strong
5.definition: Performance is consistently strong, proactive, and well controlled.

qualityScore.label: Quality performance
qualityScore.hint: Conformance, defect history, and complaint performance.

deliveryScore.label: Delivery performance
deliveryScore.hint: On-time delivery, service reliability, and schedule stability.

responsivenessScore.label: Responsiveness
responsivenessScore.hint: Speed and clarity when issues, requests, or escalations arise.

complianceScore.label: Compliance and documentation
complianceScore.hint: Certificates, approvals, and required supporting records.

traceabilityScore.label: Traceability and control
traceabilityScore.hint: Record retrieval, identification, and control of supplied product or service.

changeControlScore.label: Change notification
changeControlScore.hint: How reliably the provider communicates changes before impact occurs.

narrative.default: Annual evaluation uses one consistent questionnaire so provider review stays comparable year to year.
narrative.notRecorded: No annual evaluation has been recorded yet. Use the questionnaire to score quality, delivery, responsiveness, compliance, traceability, and change control.
narrative.approved: This provider’s latest annual evaluation is strong and supports routine approved status.
narrative.approvedWithConditions: This provider is acceptable, but the annual evaluation shows follow-up is still needed in one or more control areas.
narrative.escalated: This provider’s annual evaluation needs management attention and a clear follow-up plan.
narrative.disqualified: This provider’s annual evaluation is poor enough to question continued approval without further review.

headline.incomplete: Complete all six questions to see the live supplier-performance view.
headline.strong: Current performance looks strong and stable.
headline.acceptable: Current performance is acceptable, with some areas needing follow-up.
headline.escalated: Current performance needs escalation and a focused improvement plan.
headline.weak: Current performance is weak enough to question ongoing approval without intervention.

copy.incomplete: The live performance view appears once the annual questionnaire is fully scored.
copy.watch: The provider is generally acceptable, but follow-up is still needed in {{areas}}.
copy.strong: All scored areas are controlled or strong, which supports routine supplier review.

guidance.structured: This provider record already shows what is being supplied, why the provider is approved, who reviews it, and how the latest annual evaluation landed.
guidance.default: Record the provider, what they supply, why they are approved or conditionally accepted, who reviews the relationship, and how the annual evaluation scored them.

headline.needsAttention: This provider currently needs management attention.
headline.underControl: This provider is currently under control.

copy.unsaved: Attention guidance appears after the provider is saved.
copy.underControl: Approval status, review timing, evaluation, and supplier-audit coverage are controlled enough for routine oversight.

review.noRecord: Save the provider first, then link the process, audit, risk, action, or obligation records that show how it is controlled.
review.unlinked: This provider is recorded, but its operational control and review evidence are not yet visible through linked records.
review.auditMissing: This critical supplier has supporting links, but it still needs a linked supplier audit to complete the review picture.
review.partial: This provider has some traceability, but it will be easier to review once both the owning process and a supporting audit or control record are linked.
review.strong: This provider already shows where it is controlled and which IMS records support review and follow-up.

criticalityFocus.high: This provider supports a high-impact product, service, or outsourced activity and should stay visible in formal review.
criticalityFocus.medium: This provider has moderate impact and should stay within routine review and audit coverage.
criticalityFocus.low: This provider is recorded for visibility, with lighter follow-up expectations unless the relationship changes.

auditCoverage.title: Supplier audit expectation
auditCoverage.copy: Critical suppliers should show at least one linked supplier audit as part of formal annual oversight.

narrative.notRequired: A dedicated supplier audit is not required for this provider based on its current type and criticality.
narrative.linked: A supplier audit is already linked, so annual provider review shows formal audit support.
narrative.required: This provider is marked as a critical supplier, so link a supplier audit to complete formal annual oversight.

headline.default: Next steps appear after the provider is saved.
headline.audit: Link the required supplier audit next.
headline.action: Prepare a provider follow-up action from this review.
headline.traceability: Complete the review picture with process and audit traceability.
headline.ready: This provider review is connected and ready for routine oversight.

copy.default: Save the provider first, then decide whether the next control step is supplier audit coverage, action follow-up, or traceability completion.
copy.audit: This provider is high criticality, so annual oversight is not complete until a supplier audit is linked.
copy.action: The annual evaluation already shows follow-up is needed. Prepare an action so ownership, due date, and review stay visible in the global tracker.
copy.traceability: Link the owning process and a supporting audit so this provider review is easier to defend during management review and audit follow-up.
copy.ready: The provider already shows approval basis, annual evaluation, supplier-audit coverage, and linked operational controls in one place.

links.title: Traceability hub
links.copy: Link the existing process, audit, risk, action, and obligation records that already carry the operational follow-up.

PROCESS.title: Linked processes
PROCESS.copy: Processes that depend on or control this external provider relationship.
PROCESS.empty: Link the process that owns or relies on this provider relationship.

RISK.title: Linked risks
RISK.copy: Risks used to track dependence, continuity, quality, or control exposure for this provider.
RISK.empty: Link a risk if this provider creates continuity, quality, or compliance exposure.

AUDIT.title: Linked audits
AUDIT.copy: Audits that reviewed the provider or the process controls around this provider. High-criticality suppliers should have a linked supplier audit.
AUDIT.empty: Link an audit that checked supplier or outsourced-provider control. For high-criticality suppliers, use a supplier audit.

ACTION.title: Linked actions
ACTION.copy: Follow-up actions already tracked in the global action register.
ACTION.empty: Link actions already being tracked for provider follow-up.

OBLIGATION.title: Linked obligations
OBLIGATION.copy: Compliance or customer obligations that apply to this provider relationship.
OBLIGATION.empty: Link obligations when customer, legal, or regulatory requirements apply to this provider.

messages.noPermissionWrite: You do not have permission to edit external providers.
messages.completeRequired: Complete the required external provider fields.
messages.loadListFailed: External providers could not be loaded.
messages.loadDetailsFailed: External provider details could not be loaded.
messages.loadOwnersFailed: External provider owners could not be loaded.
messages.auditLinkNeeded: Supplier audit still needs to be linked for this critical provider.
messages.defaultDraftDescription: Follow up annual provider review for {{title}}.

descriptions.list: Keep workplace hazards, potential harm, and linked controls visible without creating a duplicate incident or risk workflow.
descriptions.create: Record the activity, hazard, potential harm, current controls, and review owner in one lightweight register.
descriptions.detail: Review the hazard, its potential harm, current controls, and linked IMS records.
descriptions.edit: Update the hazard record while keeping incidents, risks, processes, and actions in their original modules.

list.copy: Track workplace hazards, potential harm, and review ownership in one visible register.
list.loadingCopy: Refreshing hazard records, potential harm, and linked controls.
list.emptyCopy: Create the first hazard to start tracking workplace exposure visibly in the IMS.

placeholders.activity: Dispatch loading, chemical transfer, manual handling
placeholders.hazard: Pedestrian and vehicle interaction | Hose failure | Chemical splash
placeholders.potentialHarm: Describe the likely harm if the hazard is not controlled.
placeholders.existingControls: Summarize the current controls already applied for this hazard.

guidance.structured: This hazard record already shows the activity, the exposed harm, and who reviews the control position.
guidance.default: Record the activity, the hazard, the potential harm, and who reviews the current controls so the exposure stays visible.

review.noRecord: Save the hazard first, then link the process, incident, risk, or action records that show how it is being managed.
review.unlinked: This hazard is recorded, but the operational controls and event history are not yet visible through linked records.
review.partial: This hazard has some traceability, but it will be stronger once the owning process and the closest incident or near miss are both linked.
review.strong: This hazard already shows where it is controlled and where the supporting incident, risk, and action evidence sits.

detail.noPotentialHarm: No potential harm summary recorded yet.

headline.default: Next steps appear after the hazard is saved.
headline.risk: Link the formal risk assessment next.
headline.action: Prepare a hazard follow-up action next.
headline.traceability: Complete the control and event picture for this hazard.
headline.ready: This hazard is connected to the main control records.

copy.default: Save the hazard first, then decide whether the next step is risk assessment, action follow-up, or event/process traceability.
copy.risk: This hazard identifies the source of harm, but the formal assessment still needs to be visible in the Risk register.
copy.action: The hazard is already visible and high severity. Prepare an action so control improvement ownership and due dates stay visible in the global tracker.
copy.traceability: Link the owning process and any related incident or near miss so this hazard is easier to review during OH&S follow-up.
copy.ready: The hazard already shows identification, formal risk connection, event context, and action follow-up in one place.

links.title: Traceability hub
links.copy: Link the existing process, incident, risk, and action records that already carry the live follow-up.

PROCESS.title: Linked processes
PROCESS.copy: Processes that own or apply the operational control for this hazard.
PROCESS.empty: Link the process that owns the routine control for this hazard.

RISK.title: Linked risks
RISK.copy: Risks that formally assess and manage the exposure created by this hazard. The hazard stays as the identification record, while the risk holds the assessed treatment path.
RISK.empty: Link a risk when this hazard has been formally assessed in the Risk register and is being managed through the risk workflow.

ACTION.title: Linked actions
ACTION.copy: Follow-up actions already tracked in the global action register.
ACTION.empty: Link actions already being tracked for follow-up.

INCIDENT.title: Linked incidents
INCIDENT.copy: Incidents or near misses that show the hazard in a real event context.
INCIDENT.empty: Link a related incident or near miss to keep event evidence visible.

messages.riskLinkNeeded: Formal risk assessment still needs to be linked.
messages.noPermissionWrite: You do not have permission to edit hazards.
messages.loadDetailsFailed: Hazard details could not be loaded.
messages.loadOwnersFailed: Hazard owners could not be loaded.

descriptions.list: Track incidents and near misses without turning the system into a full operational workflow engine.
descriptions.create: Record what happened, immediate action, and who owns follow-up.
descriptions.detail: Review the event, response, and linked records already managing the follow-up.
descriptions.edit: Update the incident record while keeping linked processes, risks, and actions in their original modules.

list.copy: Keep incidents and near misses visible without duplicating NCR or CAPA workflows.
list.loadingCopy: Refreshing incidents, near misses, and linked follow-up records.
list.emptyCopy: Create the first incident or near miss to start tracking event follow-up visibly in the IMS.

placeholders.title: Coolant spill at line preparation area
placeholders.description: Summarize what happened and why it matters.
placeholders.immediateAction: What was done immediately to secure the situation?
placeholders.investigationSummary: What was found during review of the event?
placeholders.rootCause: Record the current root cause conclusion or use the structured RCA prompts below.
placeholders.correctiveActionSummary: What follow-up or improvement is planned?

fiveWhy.title: 5 Why prompt
fiveWhy.copy: Use the causal chain to build the investigation. If the root cause field is left blank, these answers will be combined into it on save.

fishbone.title: Fishbone prompt
fishbone.copy: Capture likely causes by category. If the root cause field is left blank, these notes will be combined into it on save.

guidance.structured: This record already shows what happened, who owns it, and what was done immediately.
guidance.default: A useful incident record should show what happened, when it happened, what was done immediately, and who owns the follow-up.

review.noRecord: Save the incident first, then link the process, risk, or action records that carry the follow-up.
review.unlinked: This event is recorded, but its operational follow-up is not yet visible through linked records.
review.partial: This event has some traceability, but it will be easier to review once both the affected process and related risk are linked.
review.strong: This event already shows where it applies and how the follow-up is being managed.

investigation.structured: The investigation now shows both the RCA method used and the root cause captured from the review.
investigation.summaryOnly: The event has investigation notes, but the root cause method still needs to be made explicit.
investigation.default: Keep the first report lightweight, then use this section to capture what was found, the RCA method used, and the resulting root cause.
investigation.none: No investigation narrative is available yet.
investigation.detailStructured: The investigation records both the RCA method used and the current root cause summary.
investigation.detailSummaryOnly: Investigation notes are recorded, but the root cause method has not been made explicit yet.
investigation.detailNone: No structured investigation has been recorded yet.

headline.default: Next steps appear after the incident is saved.
headline.action: Create follow-up from the incident once the investigation is clear.
headline.traceability: Complete the traceability picture with the affected process and related risk.
headline.ready: The incident has enough structure to manage follow-up and review.

copy.default: Save the event, then review investigation, evidence, and follow-up in one place.
copy.investigation: Use the RCA method and root cause fields to make the investigation defensible before pushing more follow-up into actions.
copy.action: If the event needs corrective or preventive work, prepare an incident action from the section below so ownership and due dates are explicit.
copy.traceability: Link the affected process and related risk so the event is visible in the wider control system, not just the incident log.
copy.ready: Review the linked action, evidence, and traceability regularly until the event can be closed confidently.

evidence.title: Reporting and investigation evidence
evidence.copy: Use one evidence area for the original report, investigation records, photos, witness notes, and follow-up support documents.

links.title: Traceability hub
links.copy: Link the existing process, risk, and action records that already carry the follow-up.

PROCESS.title: Linked processes
PROCESS.copy: Processes affected by this event or responsible for maintaining the control.
PROCESS.empty: Link the affected process so the event can be reviewed in context.

RISK.title: Linked risks
RISK.copy: Risks that reflect the exposure highlighted by this event.
RISK.empty: Link a risk if this event exposes an existing weakness or area needing treatment.

ACTION.title: Linked actions
ACTION.copy: Follow-up actions already tracked in the global action register.
ACTION.empty: Link actions already being tracked for follow-up.

headline.needsAttention: This event currently needs management attention.
headline.underControl: This event is currently under control.

copy.default: Attention guidance appears after the event is saved.
copy.underControl: Ownership, severity, and current investigation state are clear enough for routine follow-up.

messages.noPermissionWrite: You do not have permission to edit incidents.
messages.loadListFailed: Incident register could not be loaded.
messages.loadDetailsFailed: Incident details could not be loaded.
messages.loadOwnersFailed: Incident owners could not be loaded.

descriptions.list: Track legal, regulatory, contractual, and external obligations in one controlled register.
descriptions.create: Record a new obligation, assign ownership, and set the next review point.
descriptions.detail: Review ownership, review timing, linked assurance records, and follow-up actions in one place.
descriptions.edit: Update the obligation record while keeping linked audits, risks, processes, and actions intact.

list.copy: Track legal, regulatory, and external obligations in one controlled register.
list.loadingCopy: Refreshing compliance obligations and linked records.
list.emptyCopy: Create the first obligation to start tracking requirements visibly in the IMS.

guidance.structured: This obligation now has a clear source, review owner, and next review date.
guidance.default: Capture the source, ownership, and next review point first. Links can be added later when useful.

placeholders.title: Waste handling permit conditions are reviewed annually
placeholders.source: Environmental permit | Customer contract | Labour code
placeholders.description: Summarize what the organization must do or maintain.

review.noRecord: Open an obligation record to see its review position.
review.unlinked: This obligation is recorded, but it is not yet connected to the IMS records that show how it is managed.
review.partial: Some traceability is in place, but the obligation would benefit from clearer process and risk linkage.
review.strong: This obligation already has useful traceability across the system and is easier to review with confidence.

headline.default: Review the obligation and decide where it should connect next.
headline.process: Link the process that operates this obligation next.
headline.audit: Show where this obligation is checked in assurance.
headline.action: Create or link follow-up where the obligation still needs active control.
headline.done: This obligation already has a workable review trail.

copy.default: Start with the obligation owner, review date, and source. Add links only when they improve traceability or follow-up.
copy.process: Link the process that carries this obligation so ownership and operational context stay visible.
copy.audit: Add an audit link when this obligation is explicitly reviewed or tested in assurance activity.
copy.action: Add an action when this obligation still needs follow-up, implementation work, or review preparation.
copy.done: The obligation already has enough linked context to support review, assurance, and follow-up.

links.title: Traceability hub
links.copy: Link the records that show how this obligation is managed in the IMS.
links.optionalCopy: This obligation is already saved. Add process, risk, audit, or action links only when they are useful for traceability or follow-up.

PROCESS.title: Linked processes
PROCESS.copy: Processes that operate or review this obligation.
PROCESS.empty: Optional for now. Add the operating process when you want this obligation anchored to process ownership.

RISK.title: Linked risks
RISK.copy: Risks or opportunities associated with this obligation.
RISK.empty: Optional for now. Add a risk when this obligation creates exposure or opportunity.

AUDIT.title: Linked audits
AUDIT.copy: Audits that verify how this obligation is checked.
AUDIT.empty: Optional for now. Add an audit when this obligation is formally reviewed in assurance work.

ACTION.title: Linked actions
ACTION.copy: Follow-up actions that help keep this obligation under control.
ACTION.empty: Optional for now. Add an action when the obligation needs visible follow-up.

headline.needsAttention: This obligation still needs some active management attention.
headline.underControl: This obligation is currently under control.

copy.default: Review the current ownership, review timing, and linked records to decide whether follow-up is needed.
copy.underControl: The current owner, review timing, and linked records give this obligation a workable level of control.
copy.needsAttention: This obligation still needs attention around {{reasons}}.

messages.auditCoverageMissing: Audit coverage is not linked yet.
messages.noPermissionWrite: You do not have permission to update compliance obligations.
messages.linkRemoveFailed: Linked record could not be removed.
messages.loadListFailed: Compliance obligations could not be loaded.
messages.loadDetailsFailed: Compliance obligation details could not be loaded.
messages.loadOwnersFailed: Obligation owners could not be loaded.

descriptions.list: A lightweight registry for business processes and their linked IMS records.
descriptions.create: Create a process definition without changing the existing compliance modules.
descriptions.detail: Review ownership, interfaces, and linked records for this process.
descriptions.edit: Update the process definition while keeping linked records in their original modules.

list.copy: A lightweight process registry linked to existing IMS records.
list.loadingCopy: Refreshing process definitions and linked record counts.
list.emptyCopy: Create the first process record to start using this register.

DOCUMENT.title: Linked Documents
DOCUMENT.copy: Controlled documents that define or support this process.
DOCUMENT.emptyCopy: Link existing controlled documents to keep process context visible here.

RISK.title: Linked Risks
RISK.copy: Risk records currently associated with this process.
RISK.emptyCopy: Link existing risks to show where this process is exposed.

AUDIT.title: Linked Audits
AUDIT.copy: Audits that evaluate how this process is working.
AUDIT.emptyCopy: Link relevant audits so assurance activity is visible in one place.

KPI.title: Linked KPIs
KPI.copy: Performance indicators used to monitor this process.
KPI.emptyCopy: Link KPIs if you want to monitor this process from the register.

ACTION.title: Linked Actions
ACTION.copy: Actions that are already being tracked elsewhere in the IMS.
ACTION.emptyCopy: Link actions already tracked in the global action register.

NCR.title: Linked NCR / Issues
NCR.copy: Nonconformances or issues raised against this process.
NCR.emptyCopy: Link related nonconformances or issues for process-level visibility.

CONTEXT_ISSUE.title: Linked Context Issues
CONTEXT_ISSUE.copy: Internal or external issues from Clause 4 that influence this process.
CONTEXT_ISSUE.emptyCopy: Link internal or external issues so process context is visible here.

nextSteps.noneSelected: Next: review the saved process and start linking the existing IMS records that belong to it.
nextSteps.hasLinks: Next: review the linked records and add any missing risks, audits, documents, NCRs, actions, or context issues.
nextSteps.noLinks: Next: link the first related record so this process becomes a useful hub instead of a standalone definition.

guidance.definitionReady: The process definition is taking shape as a controlled record with ownership and clear interfaces.
guidance.definitionNeeded: A useful process record should show who owns it, why it exists, and what inputs and outputs define the interface.
guidance.assuranceNone: Save the process first, then link the risks, audits, documents, and actions that provide assurance around it.
guidance.assuranceNoLinks: This process is defined, but it is not yet supported by linked evidence. Add risks, audits, documents, or NCRs so the process can be reviewed in context.
guidance.assurancePartial: The process already has some linkage, but it would benefit from both risk visibility and document control evidence.
guidance.assuranceReady: This process already has linked assurance records, making it more useful for audit and management review.

start.title: How do you want to start?
start.templateCopy: Choose a standard process, preview it, then apply it to the form and edit every field before saving.
start.customCopy: Use the blank form for processes that are unique to your organization. You can still switch back to a starter later.
start.previewCopy: This only pre-fills the form. You can change every field before saving.

traceability.title: At a glance
traceability.copy: Use this process as a review hub for the records already linked to it, without changing their original workflows.

links.title: Process linkage hub
links.copy: View and connect existing IMS records without changing their original workflows.
links.emptyCopy: Link the risks, audits, documents, NCRs, or actions that belong to this process.

linkComposer.emptyCopy: There are no existing {{section}} to link yet.

messages.noPermissionWrite: You do not have permission to edit the process register.
messages.starterApplied: Starter applied from {{name}}. Review and customize the process before saving.
messages.loadListFailed: Process register could not be loaded.
messages.loadDetailsFailed: Process details could not be loaded.
messages.loadOwnersFailed: Process owners could not be loaded.

lifecycle.title: How processes interact across the IMS
lifecycle.copy: Use the process register as the spine. Planning modules define the process, operational modules run it, assurance modules test it, and improvement modules close the loop.
lifecycle.improvementBar: Continual improvement across the integrated management system
lifecycle.processCoreCopy: Each process remains the reference point for ownership, scope, interfaces, and linked evidence across the IMS.

plan.title: Plan
plan.copy: Define why the process exists, what risks surround it, which obligations apply, and how success will be measured.

do.title: Do
do.copy: Run the process with controlled documents, training, provider controls, and managed change.

check.title: Check
check.copy: Use assurance records to test whether the process performs as intended and where issues are emerging.

act.title: Act
act.copy: Turn findings and trends into corrective action, follow-up tasks, and management decisions.

descriptions.list: Run structured ISO management reviews with clear inputs, decisions, and action outputs.
descriptions.create: Capture the meeting against a structured ISO template and use live system records as evidence.
descriptions.detail: Review management inputs, outputs, and linked actions in one calm operational workspace.
descriptions.edit: Update the meeting record while preserving the action context and using live records as evidence.

list.title: Structured management reviews
list.copy: Run ISO management review meetings with explicit inputs, outputs, decisions, and action follow-up.
list.loadingCopy: Refreshing meetings, status, and recorded management-review content.

form.copy: Use the built-in ISO structure for inputs and outputs instead of a single free-form note.

readiness.inputsReady: Input coverage is strong enough for an evidence-based review.
readiness.inputsThin: Record the main audit, CAPA, KPI, provider, obligation, and risk inputs before the meeting.
readiness.outputsReady: Outputs are taking shape for controlled follow-up.
readiness.outputsThin: Decisions, system changes, objective changes, and resources should be explicit.

liveInputs.title: Live system inputs
liveInputs.copy: Use these registers as live evidence when writing the input sections below.
liveInputs.aiCopy: You can also ask AI to draft the input text from the current tenant records, then edit it before saving.
liveInputs.aiAction: AI draft inputs from live records
liveInputs.aiNote: AI fills only the management review input sections. You can edit every field before saving the meeting.

inputs.title: Inputs
inputs.copy: Use these sections to cover the main ISO management-review inputs across quality, environmental, and OH&S performance.

placeholders.capaStatus: Summarize open, overdue, and effective CAPA
placeholders.kpiPerformance: Summarize KPI performance, breaches, and trends
placeholders.customerInterestedPartiesFeedback: Customer satisfaction, complaints, stakeholder concerns, and other feedback
placeholders.providerPerformance: Supplier/provider performance, approvals, audits, and escalations
placeholders.complianceObligations: Regulatory, legal, contractual, and compliance-obligation review status
placeholders.incidentEmergencyPerformance: Incidents, near misses, emergency response, drills, and lessons learned
placeholders.consultationCommunication: Worker consultation, participation, communications, complaints, or relevant interested-party dialogue
placeholders.risksOpportunities: Summarize current risk exposure and opportunities
placeholders.changesAffectingSystem: Regulatory, organizational, supplier, or process changes
placeholders.previousActions: Status of previous management review outputs

outputs.title: Outputs
outputs.copy: Use these sections to record the formal outputs expected from management review, not just meeting notes.

placeholders.systemChangesNeeded: Needed changes to the management system, controls, or processes
placeholders.objectiveTargetChanges: Changes to objectives, targets, measures, or review priorities
placeholders.resourceNeeds: People, budget, competence, or infrastructure needs
placeholders.effectivenessConclusion: Conclusion on suitability, adequacy, and effectiveness of the management system

summary.inputsCopy: Core management review inputs are recorded directly in the meeting itself.
summary.outputsReady: Decisions and outputs are recorded for follow-up.
summary.outputsThin: Capture decisions, system changes, objective changes, and resources before closure.

attention.title: Complete the meeting record before raising actions
attention.copy: This review still needs written meeting content. Use Edit meeting first, then create actions from the sections that contain actual decisions or follow-up needs.
attention.note: Action buttons stay available only where section content already exists.

liveSignals.title: Live review signals
liveSignals.copy: These records should inform the meeting narrative, but they do not write into the review automatically. Use them when updating audit results, risks and opportunities, and changes affecting the system.

readinessHints.closed: A closed review should show decisions, system changes, objective changes, resources, and an effectiveness conclusion.
readinessHints.held: Held meetings should already show minutes, decisions, initial actions, and management direction for follow-up.
readinessHints.planned: Planned meetings should bring together audit, KPI, provider, compliance, incident, and risk inputs before discussion starts.

managementPositionHints.pending: Management review outputs will appear once the meeting record is saved.
managementPositionHints.closed: The meeting can be closed even while resulting actions continue afterward, but the conclusion and resource position should be explicit.
managementPositionHints.held: Review the decisions, output actions, and effectiveness direction before treating this meeting as closed.
managementPositionHints.planned: Use the live system records below to prepare evidence before the meeting is held.

customerFeedback.label: Customer feedback
customerFeedback.copyWithResponses: {{count}} completed survey responses with an average of {{average}}/10. {{attention}} responses sit in the 0-6 attention range.
customerFeedback.copyEmpty: No completed customer surveys are recorded yet, so this section may still rely on NCRs, complaints, and direct stakeholder discussion.
customerFeedback.reviewCopy: Use customer survey scores, low-score comments, complaints, and other interested-party feedback when writing this section.

auditSupplier.label: Audit and supplier assurance
auditSupplier.copy: {{incidents}} open incidents and {{providers}} provider review items should inform audit results and supplier-control discussion.
auditSupplier.reviewCopy: Use these live records when summarising audit results and supplier assurance themes.

riskCompliance.label: Risk and compliance exposure
riskCompliance.copy: {{obligations}} obligation review items, {{hazards}} high hazards, and {{aspects}} significant aspects should inform risk and opportunity review.
riskCompliance.reviewCopy: Use these live records when writing risks and opportunities and wider compliance exposure.

changes.label: Changes affecting the system
changes.copy: {{count}} active change requests may need to be discussed as current system change.
changes.reviewCopy: Use active change requests to support the changes affecting the system section.

messages.noPermissionWrite: You do not have permission to update management reviews.
messages.completeRequired: Complete the required management review fields.
messages.aiAddonOff: AI assistant add-on is not enabled for this tenant.
messages.aiReplaceConfirm: Replace the current management review input text with a new AI draft from live records?
messages.aiDrafted: Management review inputs drafted from live records using {{provider}} {{model}}.
messages.noPermissionActions: You do not have permission to create actions.
messages.followUpOpened: Follow-up action draft opened below from {{section}}.
messages.defaultAgenda: Review performance of the integrated management system.
messages.addContentTooltip: Add meeting content first, then prepare an action from that section.
messages.prepareActionTooltip: Prepare a follow-up action from this section.
messages.archiveConfirm: Archive this management review? It will be removed from the active meeting list but kept in the audit trail.
messages.pdfFailed: Management review PDF could not be prepared.
messages.pptFailed: Management review PPT could not be prepared.
messages.loadDetailsFailed: Management review details could not be loaded.
messages.loadListFailed: Management reviews could not be loaded.

descriptions.list: A focused list of KPI definitions and current status against target.
descriptions.create: Define a KPI in its own page without mixing reading history into the same screen.
descriptions.detail: Review current status, thresholds, and reading history in one clean KPI record.
descriptions.edit: Adjust the KPI definition in a dedicated editing workflow.

list.title: KPI register
list.copy: Track current performance, targets, thresholds, and watch or breach states in one calm list.

form.copy: Define target logic cleanly here. Readings and trend history stay on the KPI detail page.
form.guidance: Use the warning threshold to define when management attention should start before a full breach occurs.

directionGuidance.atLeast: Use this for metrics such as on-time delivery, training completion, or compliance rate where performance improves as the result goes up.
directionGuidance.atMost: Use this for metrics such as complaints, defects, waste, or incident rate where performance improves as the result goes down.

performance.defaultNarrative: Review the KPI against target, warning threshold, and recent readings.
performance.narrativeBreach: This KPI is outside the accepted performance band for {{period}} review. The management response should confirm cause, corrective action, and follow-up timing.
performance.narrativeWatch: This KPI is still within the control range but close enough to the warning threshold that management follow-up is appropriate before performance deteriorates further.
performance.narrativeOnTarget: This KPI is meeting the expected target direction. Continue routine monitoring and keep the reading history current as evidence of sustained performance.
performance.defaultHint: Save the KPI to start recording evidence-based readings.
performance.hintBreach: Next step: update the latest reading notes with cause and planned response.
performance.hintWatch: Next step: add the latest reading notes and confirm whether intervention is needed.
performance.hintOnTarget: Next step: keep the next planned reading on schedule to demonstrate ongoing control.

readings.title: Readings
readings.copy: Add current readings and keep the recent evidence trail visible below.

interpretation.withinLimit: Reading stayed within the target limit.

messages.detailLoadFailed: KPI details could not be loaded.

page.descriptionList: Use NCR to record the nonconformance, who owns it, what follow-up is needed, and whether verification is complete.
page.descriptionCreate: Capture the nonconformance first, then continue the investigation and corrective trail from the record.
page.descriptionDetail: Review the nonconformance, investigation, follow-up actions, verification, comments, attachments, and activity.
page.descriptionEdit: Update the NCR while keeping the investigation and verification trail clear.

list.title: Nonconformance log
list.copy: NCR records the nonconformance first, then keeps the investigation, action follow-up, and verification trail together.
list.filtersCopy: Filter by status, category, source, severity, or owner.

empty.noAccessCopy: Your current role does not include ncr.read.
empty.noneTitle: No NCR records match the current filter
empty.noneCopy: Adjust the filters or create the first NCR entry for this tenant.

placeholders.title: Unapproved process change
placeholders.description: Describe what happened and what requirement was missed

detail.workflowCopy: Work from investigation to task follow-up and then formal verification before closure.

guidance.ownerFilterDefault: Select a process if you want to suggest the process owner as the most likely NCR owner.
guidance.ownerFilterScoped: Owner options are narrowed to {{owner}} from {{process}}.
guidance.ownerFilterNoOwner: This process does not have an assigned process owner yet, so all users remain available.

nextSteps.create: Next: open the saved NCR and continue with investigation, actions, and verification from the record.
nextSteps.edit: Next: continue the NCR from the relevant workflow tab.
nextSteps.initial: Next: continue the investigation, capture root cause, and then move into corrective actions.
nextSteps.actioning: Next: review corrective actions, keep comments and evidence current, and prepare for verification.
nextSteps.verification: Next: complete verification and confirm whether the NCR can be closed or needs more action.
nextSteps.closed: Next: review the investigation trail, linked actions, and evidence as part of the NCR record.

guidance.initial: Capture containment first, then record enough investigation detail to justify why the NCR is moving into root cause analysis.
guidance.investigation: Complete the investigation summary and root cause before moving fully into corrective action ownership.
guidance.correctiveAction: Keep corrective action summary, ownership, comments, and evidence current so the NCR is ready for verification.
guidance.verification: Use verification to confirm the corrective action was effective, not just completed. Reject verification if the issue can still recur.
guidance.closed: Keep the NCR record available as evidence of containment, root cause, corrective action, and verification outcome.
guidance.archived: Archived NCRs remain available for traceability and should not be treated as active issues.

guidance.verified: The corrective action has been confirmed as effective. Keep the verifier and verification date visible in the record.
guidance.rejected: Use rejected when the corrective action did not prevent recurrence. Return to investigation or action in progress with updated evidence.
guidance.pending: Pending means the action may be implemented, but effectiveness has not yet been confirmed.

followUp.overdueCopy: The due date passed on {{dueDate}}. Review corrective actions, verification status, and owner accountability before the NCR drifts further.
followUp.approachingCopy: The due date is {{dueDate}}. Confirm the investigation, root cause, and corrective action trail before the due date arrives.
followUp.verificationHeading: Verification still needs a formal result
followUp.verificationCopy: This NCR is pending verification. Record who verified the action and whether the result was accepted or rejected.

headlines.needsAttention: This NCR currently needs management attention.
headlines.underControl: This NCR is currently under control.

narratives.unsaved: Attention guidance appears after the NCR is saved.
narratives.underControl: Ownership, due date, verification state, and current NCR stage are controlled enough for routine follow-up.

messages.deleteConfirmCurrent: Delete this NCR? Closed records should be archived instead.

page.descriptionList: Use CAPA for formal corrective action: define the issue, confirm root cause, assign action, verify effectiveness, and close with evidence.
page.descriptionCreate: Create a CAPA record for issues that need formal corrective action and tracked closure.
page.descriptionDetail: Review the problem, root cause, actions, verification, and closure evidence in one place.
page.descriptionEdit: Update the CAPA record while keeping the corrective-action trail clear.

list.title: CAPA register
list.copy: CAPA manages root cause, corrective action, verification, and closure after a significant issue is raised.
list.filtersCopy: Search by title or source, then open the record for cause analysis, action follow-up, and closure evidence.

empty.loadingCopy: Refreshing nonconformity, corrective action, and closure status.
empty.noneTitle: No CAPA records match the current filter
empty.noneCopy: Adjust the search or create the first CAPA entry for this tenant.

form.copy: Keep the problem statement, cause, actions, and closure inputs in clearly separated sections.

headline.OPEN: Start by defining the issue clearly
headline.IN_PROGRESS: Keep ownership and due dates visible
headline.VERIFIED: Confirm the action worked before closure
headline.CLOSED: Closure should be supported by a clear summary

copy.OPEN: Record the problem clearly so the team understands what happened and why formal corrective action is needed.
copy.INVESTIGATING: Containment and root cause should be clear before the CAPA moves into action planning.
copy.ACTION_PLANNED: The corrective response should now be clear enough to assign, track, and review.
copy.IN_PROGRESS: Once actions are underway, owner accountability and due-date control become the main focus.
copy.VERIFIED: Verification should confirm that the action was effective, not only completed.
copy.CLOSED: A closed CAPA should explain what evidence supports closure and why recurrence is now controlled.

hint.assigned: Owner already assigned. Keep the due date and verification method aligned with the selected status.
hint.unassigned: Assign an owner once the CAPA moves beyond initial definition so accountability is visible.

actionControl.noOwnerNoDate: Assign an owner and due date once the CAPA moves into action planning so follow-up is visible.
actionControl.noOwner: Due date is set, but owner is still missing. Assign ownership before the CAPA relies on action follow-up.
actionControl.noDueDate: Owner is assigned, but due date is still missing. Add a due date so the CAPA can be monitored properly.
actionControl.ready: Owner and due date are set. Keep them aligned with the current CAPA stage and any linked tasks.

headline.needsAttention: This CAPA currently needs management attention.
headline.underControl: This CAPA is currently under control.

narrative.unsaved: Attention guidance appears after the CAPA is saved.
narrative.underControl: Ownership, due date, verification, and current CAPA stage are controlled enough for routine follow-up.

messages.noPermission: You do not have permission to update CAPA records.
messages.deleteConfirm: Delete this CAPA? Closed CAPA records cannot be deleted.
